author: @Heartbreath333
text: Can't believe this wonderful man has gone....when COVID was on the go his videos inspired me to make things ...he was such a Wealth of information...rest in peace Robert..

author: @Brick_Bat
text: RIP Rob, you were awesome in so many ways

author: @Thingssimplyfied
text:
We watch the sun go down, and suddenly the world feels darker than we expected.
I will miss you deeply, Robert. Thank you for sharing your profound knowledge.
You inspired and empowered all who had the privilege of listening to you.
Your spirit remains with us.

author: @xclusive2b
text: I came across his brother's last and was so sad! Love his videos, especially his designs for heaters! will missed his bright laugh! May he rest in peace! Surely I will keep coming back to this channel since there are still too many great things to see and learn!

author: @SeanFrancis-d8c
text: Thank you Robert. May your soul be at peace

author: @TMGpuntNL
text:
why the heck does it hurt so much to know we have lost a guy called Robert Murray-Smith while most of us only know him from the videos he made?

It brings me to tears. I watched almost all if not all of his videos. But i had some stuff in personal life which made it impossible for me to keep track of some of the latest videos. Like say, half a year ago or so i had to stop watching a bit.
And now i was recommended a video by youtube, about a diesel heater. 
And so i thought: heck, let me check what else he has made in the last few months.
And then you see video 2440.

And the video right after made by his brother.

It breaks my heart to have learned we have just lost a great human being with a huge heart.

For some reason it feels unreal to me. 
If you have watched him for ages, and took the time to watch all of his videos like i have, it feels like a personal loss.
The man was, is, like family.

Rest in Peace, Brother from a different mother and father.
You will be missed very much.

author: @libertariamemes
text: Tu for all rip

author: @Frossglass
text: I will miss you forever, thanks for your hard work, I follow you since 2014 and you were a great inspiration for me, my favourite youtuber

author: @lawrencewatt6897
text:
Thank you Robert.
RiP and God bless you.❤

author: @LurrusMeow
text: I just wish we had you in this world just 1 more day, this world needed you my brother. Rest in Peace.

author: @duncancremin1708
text:
Oh, Robert, you couldn’t ever understand the difference you made, carrying me through my darkest times, with your interesting take on principles or problems I hadn’t paid attention to, until you nudged me, giving me a new focus just when I needed it.
I’m so sorry I couldn’t return the favour. Rest in peace.

author: @TRIEDZIIDONO
text:
Its not fair that we can hear you, & know you are gone.
RIP dear friend.

author: @normandothegreat
text: I could never get that whole 3D printer thing, honestly, that part of my brain is damaged from an accident. One thing I know for sure, is tha if anyone could have taught me how, it would have been Rob! We will miss his enthusiasm, optimism, and overall GREATNESS!!! Rest well dear Sir, no more pain my friend. 🕯😔❤️🙏

author: @secretbassrigs
text: Thank you for sharing so much with us Robert. You will be missed, but not forgotten, future generations will continue to benefit and thrive because of you and the amazing life you shared with us. 🕊🌅

author: @Ian-gr1ng
text: Thank you for everything Robert, rest in peace ❤

author: @chris993361
text: Instead of moving the Helix you could also start a sketch and then change it to be attached to the point at the end of the Helix and it will be normal to the end and update wherever the Helix is. Takes a couple steps that are not easy to explain in a YouTube comment but knowing it exists if you get curious you can look up how to do it. But you can attach a sketch to almost anything.
